content: Biological agents (viruses and bacteria, from Topic B) evolve their adaptive long-horizon survival strategies via a working-memory-like retention mechanism: they retain molecular 'memories' of past host environments to improve future infection outcomes, mirroring how active learning agents retain critical information across long timelines to update decision-making policies. This frames biological agent adaptive behavior as a natural analogue of engineered long-horizon active learning with working memory.
source_topics: Working memory retention mechanisms for long-horizon active learning
trigger_topic: Working memory retention mechanisms for long-horizon active learning
